covered_sentences: On the morning of a fine June day, the last of the Earnshaw family was born, and I was his nurse.
She had been ill with tuberculosis for a long time.
But he was the finest, healthiest, handsomest baby I had ever seen.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 2:1 (right panel larger as main panel). Left panel: camera positioned at low angle at floor level near the left wall, tilted upward about 15 degrees, looking toward the housekeeper standing in the center of the room; the iron grate fireplace is visible in the upper-right background of this panel; the stone corridor door frame is visible at the left edge as foreground. Right panel: camera positioned at medium-close range slightly above the housekeeper's right shoulder, looking down at the swaddled infant at chest height; the stone wall texture is the only background visible at the edges. Per-panel character budget: left panel 1 recognizable figure (housekeeper), right panel 0 recognizable figures (infant shown as prop bundle, housekeeper arms as foreground edge only).

Left panel: the housekeeper stands in the center of the stone hall floor, both arms cradling a tightly wrapped linen bundle (newborn), her posture protective and upright, face turned three-quarters toward the fireplace; a cloud-shaped thought bubble floats to her upper left containing a small sketch of a bed with rumpled sheets and a cross symbol — absorbing the mother's death and illness without opening a new panel. Right panel: the swaddled newborn's face is visible above the white linen wrap, cheeks full and rosy, eyes gently closed, expression peaceful and healthy; the housekeeper's forearm edge is visible at the left border of the panel as a scale reference.

Wuthering Heights main hall, early June morning, warm low-angled daylight through the deep narrow stone window; in the left panel the large stone-framed fireplace with iron grate is visible in the background right, the oak dresser with tin plates stands at the left corner, pale stone floor extends to the foreground; the housekeeper stands on the stone floor approximately one body-length from the fireplace hearth. Right panel: only the stone wall lower edge and the housekeeper's forearm are visible as the tight frame closes on the infant's face.

Warm amber morning light throughout; left panel bathed in gentle golden window light; right panel uses a soft, slightly cooler highlight focused on the infant's face, with the housekeeper's white apron providing reflected warmth. Overall palette: warm gold and pale stone.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face, dark domestic work dress, white apron, tied collar; standing in left panel — her head top reaches approximately the height of the fireplace mantelpiece visible in the upper background; her arms extend forward at approximately chest height holding the linen bundle. Proportion anchor: housekeeper head top near fireplace mantelpiece level; infant bundle spans roughly from her elbow to her wrist, infant face approximately the size of her palm.

covered_sentences: I took care of the child as if he were my own.
Hindley was satisfied just to see that he was healthy.
He had never had any love in his heart for anyone except himself and his wife.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Left-right two-panel split, ratio 1:1 (equal weight: nurturing presence vs cold indifference are equally important narrative focal points). Left panel: camera positioned to the right of the fireplace area at chest height, angled 30 degrees left-of-front, medium shot looking toward the housekeeper bending over the cradle; the fireplace iron grate is visible in the left background, a wooden cradle placed approximately one arm-length from the hearth edge is in the center. Right panel: camera positioned near the left corridor door, angled 20 degrees right-of-front, medium shot looking toward Hindley standing near the door frame; the stone door frame is visible at the left edge as foreground, the small deep-set stone window is partially visible in the upper background right.

Left panel: the housekeeper leans over a wooden cradle, both hands gently adjusting the infant's linen wrapping, her posture absorbed and tender, face in three-quarter view toward the cradle; no speech bubble — her undivided attention conveys sole care. Right panel: Hindley stands near the corridor side door, arms loosely folded at chest height, head slightly turned to glance toward the left panel direction; expression flat and indifferent, not approaching; a crossed-heart simple sketch bubble floats above his head (crossed-out heart sketch = love only for himself and his late wife, absorbing the emotional meaning without text).

Wuthering Heights main hall, daytime diffuse light; left panel: wooden cradle positioned near the fireplace, fireplace stone arch visible in the upper-left background, housekeeper crouching or leaning — her head top at approximately the level of the fireplace mantelpiece; right panel: Hindley near the corridor side door at the right-rear of the room, the door frame visibly taller than his head, small deep-set window visible above-right.

Left panel: warm amber daylight and faint fireplace warmth, soft golden tones suggesting care and comfort; right panel: cooler neutral grey-stone ambient light near the corridor, muted and detached. Color temperature contrast reinforces emotional opposition.

Housekeeper: deep brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face, dark domestic dress with white apron, tied collar; leaning over cradle — head top approximately level with the fireplace mantelpiece height in the background. Hindley: light brown or dark brown short hair, worn rough face, dark old coat and trousers, slightly disheveled shirt; standing near corridor door — head top clearly below the door frame above him, roughly the same height as the housekeeper but seen from a distance. Proportion anchor: housekeeper head near fireplace mantelpiece level; Hindley head clearly below the stone door frame lintel.

covered_sentences: Now she was gone, and he spent his time drinking and cursing.
He was so offensive that soon all the other servants left except for Joseph and me.
I did not want to leave the baby, and Joseph was always content to be where there was plenty of evil to reprove.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Three-panel horizontal sequence, equal width thirds. Left panel: camera at low floor-level angle, looking up-left toward the fireplace area, about 10 degrees upward tilt; the fireplace iron grate is in the background, its stone arch framing the upper portion; foreground has the iron grate right edge visible at the left frame edge. Center panel: camera positioned at the main entrance exterior, angled 45 degrees from outside looking inward at the doorway, mild downward angle; the stone door frame fills the left foreground edge; the stone courtyard floor and stone wall are visible in the background beyond the door. Right panel: camera at medium-close range from slightly left, angled 15 degrees right-of-front, looking toward the housekeeper (left) and Joseph (right) standing side by side near the oak dresser; the oak dresser's left edge is visible in the right background foreground. Per-panel budget: left 1 recognizable (Hindley), center 0 recognizable (departing servants shown as unrecognizable silhouette group), right 2 recognizable (housekeeper + Joseph).

Left panel: Hindley slumped in a wooden hard chair near the fireplace, right hand gripping a dark glass bottle held loosely at knee level, posture collapsed and aggressive, mouth open as if mid-curse; a storm-cloud-with-lightning-bolt sketch bubble floats above him expressing rage and cursing. Center panel: a group of servant silhouettes carrying bundles or bags (indistinct shapes, no recognizable faces, clothing indicated only by color blocks) moving toward the entrance door and out; the door frame is clearly taller than the tallest silhouette by half a head; the stone courtyard is visible beyond. Right panel: the housekeeper stands on the left, arms gently wrapped around the infant linen bundle at chest level (infant as prop), gaze directed slightly toward the center panel but feet firmly planted; Joseph stands to her right, slightly stooped, hands folded at his abdomen, lips pressed in a stern disapproving line; a pointing-finger sketch thought bubble floats above Joseph's head indicating his readiness to reprove evil.

Wuthering Heights main hall, late afternoon, dim interior light — fireplace in the left panel has low embers casting uneven orange glow; raw timber ceiling beams with hanging ham and mutton legs visible overhead in all three panels; corridor side door visible in right panel background, closed. Left panel: Hindley in wooden chair, the chair seat at approximately fireplace hearth ledge level; right panel: housekeeper and Joseph near the oak dresser, the dresser top with tin plates visible approximately one head-height above Joseph's stooped head.

Left panel: dim amber-orange uneven firelight, shadow cast upward on Hindley's face; center panel: cooler outdoor grey light from the entrance backlighting the departing silhouettes; right panel: warmer neutral light near the oak dresser, slightly cooler at Joseph's side. Overall palette muted and oppressive, desaturated browns and greys.

Hindley: light brown or dark brown short hair, worn rough face, dark old coat and trousers, disheveled open collar; slumped in wooden chair with chair seat at approximately fireplace hearth ledge height, chair back reaching to his shoulder blade level when seated. Housekeeper: deep brown hair pinned up, round kind face, dark domestic dress white apron; standing upright with infant bundle in arms. Joseph: white sparse hair, deeply lined stern face, short white beard, thin stooped frame, dark coarse long coat; stooped posture brings him to approximately the same height as the housekeeper; dresser top approximately one head above his stooped head height. Proportion anchor: Hindley seated with chair seat near fireplace hearth ledge level; Joseph stooped with dresser top approximately one head above him; housekeeper and Joseph roughly the same visible height.

covered_sentences: Hindley's treatment of Heathcliff was enough to turn a saint into a devil, and I believe there was something diabolical about Heathcliff at that time.
He enjoyed watching Hindley degrade himself.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Single panel, diagonal composition: the visual axis runs from lower-left (Hindley, degraded) to upper-right (Heathcliff, watching coldly). Camera positioned at mid-height slightly right of center looking left-across the room, framing both figures along the diagonal. Heathcliff occupies the upper-right area of the frame, standing near the right wall; Hindley occupies the lower-left midground near the fireplace. The raw timber beams overhead with hanging cured meats and the pale stone floor channel the diagonal compression. Low-angle upward tilt of about 20 degrees for Hindley's slumped figure at lower-left; Heathcliff's figure is seen from a slightly lower angle making him loom. Proportion planning: Heathcliff in upper-right occupies approximately the upper two-thirds of frame height, Hindley collapsed at lower-left with his head at about one-third frame height. Single panel — no outer border.

Heathcliff stands or leans against the right stone wall, arms loosely crossed, dark sharp eyes fixed on Hindley with cold controlled satisfaction — a faint grim trace on his lips; no speech bubble, expression carries the entire emotional weight. Hindley is slumped forward near the fireplace in the lower-left, the dark bottle on the stone floor beside the chair, posture defeated and diminished. The diagonal tension between Heathcliff's stillness and Hindley's dissolution is the core visual message; no text bubbles needed.

Wuthering Heights main hall, night or very late evening; fireplace embers provide the only warm orange glow at lower-left — the only light source; the rest of the hall is in deep shadow, stone walls barely visible, oak dresser a dark shape in the right background; raw timber ceiling beams with hanging cured meats form a low dark canopy overhead; the pale stone floor catches a faint orange pool near the hearth. Environment state: night, fireplace ember glow only, oppressive darkness throughout — corresponding to the hell-like atmosphere described. The iron grate fireplace is visible at Hindley's back, its ember light casting upward shadow on his collapsed face.

Deep shadow overall, strong orange-black contrast; ember firelight from lower-left casts dramatic upward shadows on Hindley's collapsed form, while Heathcliff in upper-right is touched only by faint ember reflection at the edge of his face — making his expression cold and ominous. Palette: charcoal black, ember orange, cold stone grey.

Heathcliff: dark brown skin, black short slightly disheveled hair, black sharp deep-set eyes, tall solid frame, dark gentleman coat, white shirt visible at collar, dark trousers, leather boots; standing in upper-right — his head top approximately half a head above the stone door frame lintel visible at the right wall edge; his figure occupies the upper two-thirds of frame height. Hindley: light brown or dark brown short hair, worn rough face, dark old coat disheveled, open collar; collapsed near fireplace at lower-left — slumped posture makes him appear significantly compressed, head at approximately one-third frame height, fireplace hearth ledge roughly at his shoulder level when collapsed. Proportion anchor: Heathcliff head top slightly above stone door frame lintel; Hindley collapsed with fireplace hearth ledge at approximately his shoulder level; Heathcliff visually dominates the upper frame while Hindley is diminished in the lower frame.
